title: Malformed `documentation` URL in charmcraft.yaml (missing `canonical/` org segment -> broken link)
summary: charmcraft.yaml line 10 sets `documentation: https://github.com/o11y-tester-operator/README.md`. This URL is missing the `canonical/` organisation segment and points to a nonexistent GitHub path (there is no `github.com/o11y-tester-operator` org). The sibling links in the same file are correctly formed with the org: `source: https://github.com/canonical/o11y-tester-operator` (line 14) and `issues: https://github.com/canonical/o11y-tester-operator/issues` (line 16). The documentation link is what Charmhub surfaces, so it currently 404s.
why_good_first_issue: One-line fix in a YAML metadata file; the correct value is obvious from the adjacent source/issues URLs in the same file. No code, no tests, immediately verifiable by opening the URL.
repro_steps: grep -n 'github.com' charmcraft.yaml -> line 10 documentation URL lacks 'canonical/'; lines 14 and 16 include it. Visiting https://github.com/o11y-tester-operator/README.md returns 404 (no such org/repo path).
repro_result: CONFIRMED via inspection. charmcraft.yaml:10 = 'documentation: https://github.com/o11y-tester-operator/README.md' (no org). Contrast charmcraft.yaml:14 = 'https://github.com/canonical/o11y-tester-operator' and :16 = 'https://github.com/canonical/o11y-tester-operator/issues', both correctly including 'canonical/'. The documentation host 'github.com/o11y-tester-operator' is not a valid GitHub org/repo path.
fix_hint: Change charmcraft.yaml:10 to point at the real README, e.g. `documentation: https://github.com/canonical/o11y-tester-operator/blob/main/README.md`.
notes: Self-identified (repo has 0 open issues). Python Juju charm; tox.ini present. Verified against current main (depth-80 clone). No outbound writes performed.

title: tox `static` env doesn't run a static type checker (it duplicates `lint`); pyright & codespell dev-deps are unused and [tool.pyright] pythonVersion contradicts requires-python
summary: Three related tooling inconsistencies: (1) the `[testenv:static]` env in tox.ini runs `uv run ... ruff check {all_path}` -- exactly the same command as `[testenv:lint]` -- so the 'static' env performs linting, not static type-checking. (2) `pyright` and `codespell` are declared in pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies].dev (lines 16,18) and pyright even has a [tool.pyright] config block (line 50), but neither tool is invoked by any tox env or any local config (grep finds no pyright/codespell command in tox.ini). (3) [tool.pyright] sets `pythonVersion = "3.8"` (line 52) while the project sets `requires-python = "==3.10.*"` (line 6) -- so even if pyright ran, it would type-check against the wrong Python version.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained tooling hygiene with a clear correct answer: make `static` actually run pyright (and optionally add a codespell step), and align [tool.pyright] pythonVersion with requires-python (3.10). Touches only tox.ini and pyproject.toml; easy to verify by running `tox -e static`. Teaches the contributor how lint/static/dev-deps are wired.
repro_steps: 1) Read tox.ini: [testenv:lint] and [testenv:static] both run `uv run {uv_flags} ruff check {all_path}` (identical). 2) grep -rin 'pyright\|codespell' tox.ini -> no command invocations (only present in pyproject.toml). 3) Compare pyproject.toml:6 requires-python='==3.10.*' vs :52 [tool.pyright] pythonVersion='3.8'.
repro_result: CONFIRMED via inspection. tox.ini [testenv:static] command == [testenv:lint] command (`uv run --frozen --isolated --all-groups --all-extras ruff check <src> <tests>`); no pyright/mypy in either. grep across tox.ini/charmcraft.yaml shows pyright & codespell appear ONLY in pyproject.toml (deps + [tool.pyright]); they are never executed by any tox env or local tooling. pyproject.toml:6 requires-python='==3.10.*' but pyproject.toml:52 [tool.pyright] pythonVersion='3.8'. (Note: pytest asyncio_mode='auto' IS valid here because pytest-operator pulls in pytest-asyncio -- not part of this finding.)
fix_hint: In tox.ini change [testenv:static] to `uv run {[vars]uv_flags} pyright {[vars]src_path}` (so 'static' really runs the static type checker that's already a dev dep). Update pyproject.toml [tool.pyright] pythonVersion from '3.8' to '3.10' to match requires-python. Optionally wire codespell into lint (add a `uv run {[vars]uv_flags} codespell ...` step) or drop codespell from dev deps if unused.
notes: Self-identified (0 open issues). Unit env builds: `tox -e unit` => 2 passed, 82% coverage (Python 3.10.20). Running `tox -e static` today only runs ruff. No outbound writes performed.
